item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm a little late to the party, but oh man did I enjoy this. I love reading fun exploration logs like the kind featured in this scp, and the fact that it was an extradimensional store run by dado (of all people) just made it better! I also loved all the little references peppered in here and there, like the man in the suit from 507 (just imagining something like that shopping at a grocery store is so funny to me!) but they might be a little confusing for newer readers (nothing wrong with that though, I think the crosslinks are quite handy just in that kind of situation.) The horrible audio file was just the icing on the cake.

Anyway tldr, loved your take on the dado format and the exploration log! +1'd for sure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love dado stuff so much

This is friggen high art here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I trust dado

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This article is a fucking gem. The audio log makes it all the more better.

+1, would upvote it again if I could.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If it is cheap enough? Yes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I never thought that I would get Rickrolled in a SCP article, but I guess it is just a matter of time, take the upvote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was going to give this a no vote. Then dado fucking rickrolled!
+1 just for this

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
dado private prison

yes, please. dado horror story and also unintentional funny story wil be good. thank you.

EDIT: ok, I spent more than five minutes thinking about this and it's probably an awful idea.

item.title: fine dado comment and useful independent review
item.content:encoded:
It always cracks me up when dado names one of his… "Double stores"? Don't know a better term. Like "vhs rental and short term loan", "private prison and gucci knock-off store" and the like.

And hey, didn't know I could immediately get an obscure reference to 507 :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My trust in dado remains unbroken.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I want dado to sing at my funeral, and i want him to sing asia by toto we are not in kansas anymore

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The audio file made me upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Despite the label, the cereal does not contain any chocolate, only a small note which reads 'real cocoa was inside you all along also hamster ate cocoa'.

Goddamnit, +1ed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am in fact Canadian, and I've seen these my entire life at Loblaws.

If you ever get into an argument with someone about whether or not Canada is socialist, just show them a No Name product and ask if a capitalist market could ever create such a thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This reminds me of "no name", basically the biggest generic brand in Canada. All-lowercase, black basic font on plain yellow packaging where it just says the product. "green beans", "fabric softener", etc. you'll see aisles of yellow in value supermarkets. It's probably what you're thinking of.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
he uses unicode

trust dado yes

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: its a big monster thing that comes out of the sea and destroys any cities that get too large or too technologically advanced and can only be calmed by the sacrifice of a mage, but that's not important right now…

item.title: Re: +1
item.content:encoded: they should at least pay the nine bucks or so they owe him for the soup, even though they had nothing to do with the damage caused by the clowns and have the video footage to prove it. he needs that money to protect against too many bird.

item.title: Re: +1
item.content:encoded: or just 'trust dado' in rainbow comic sans. no larger than 72, maybe 48 point.

item.title: Re: r.i.p.
item.content:encoded: that part alone has me wondering if dado may be a refugee from the 90s usenet boom and the days of alt.guinea-pig.conspiracy … wheeeeeeeeek

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: you can confirm or dispel something for me then - was it actually taken to its absurdist conclusion by someone selling tins simply labelled 'food'… [question mark, capitalize key is broken] … something which i've used as a spoof of value brands in photoshops before but was never sure of the truth of…

item.title: Re: u trust upvote
item.content:encoded: dados'r'us

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "three puppies fighting in a leather satchel"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
new information for the headcanon - dado is producing all his typewritten material on an atari st, or other such machine which includes ( and ) on the numpad. maybe a falcon or tt so that it has just enough umph to run a web browser and full colour dtp package.

i mean, it would totally fit the pattern of loopiness. though how he installed comic sans onto it remains unexplained.

alternatively, he only says his 'capitalize' key is broken. if he's half the caricature of a hapless computer noob that he seems to be, he exclusively uses the caps lock key to access upper case letters, and doesn't even realise that the shifts can be used for that… but he *does* know that you can use them in combination with the number keys (and maybe comma, period, etc) to gain access to a few extra symbols like ! or $ or ? … how else is he going to write out his prices? and they're written there on the keys in a position that suggests additional movement in the direction of the shift arrows, whilst the letter keys only have the uppercase forms drawn on them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Gotta be honest, I'm novoting but reeeeeeally unsure if I don't want to maybe just maybe tick that upvote.

The main issue for me is the exploration log, which just goes on and on and on, shoving as much random references in as possible. I dunno if that's all your own stuff, but I hope not.

Then again, you featured Squeedle-Deedle-Dee, so it can't all be bad.

And "Cream of Trilobite Soup" had me laughing hard enough to bother my cat. Which wasn't hard, he was sitting in my lap.

But I dunno.

item.title: night vale crossover?
item.content:encoded: is that ok within the scp-verse? is it okay to reference stuff from another franchise in scp stories?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for telling me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: dreadlordhg, instead of posting multiple comments in succession, please edit your previous post using the "edit" function under the "options" tab to the lower right of every comment. That prevents spam buildup, and it's in the rules.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: On a side note, dado sounds exactly how I though he would lol

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is simultaneously the worst and best thing I have ever read, I love it

item.title: r.i.p.
item.content:encoded:
"toys r us they not good business people like dado"
oh the pain this causes me.
also this is pure gold. dado at his finest, and laziest. there are so many good lines in this, it cracks me up so much. i love the fact that dado has guinea pigs and robots programmed to keep peeps from bad-mouthing him in his own store.

there is no way this couldn't get +1, at least in my book.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think it's the fact that it kinda breaks the flow if you haven't read the crosslinks. If you read and remember the referenced article you're good, but if you didn't or don't remember the specific part that's being referenced, then you kinda feel forced to read those in order to understand the article fully. At least, that's generally more or less my gut reaction. I have enjoyed articles without bothering to read the references, but it's always been in the back of my head whenever I do so.

I will mention that I prefer this method of crosslinking much more than name dropping the SCP in-universe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Baryon soup: 100% free range Protons

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Asia by Toto we're not in Kansas anymore

… +1
I want a t-shirt that says 'u trust dado go'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wouldn't it be easier to reference the concepts and material but force the reader to make the extremely specific concepts themselves?

In any case, crosslinks that are actually requsite reading are different from cameos, and it should generally be clear from context which is which. A cameo crosslink can be removed and make some sense in context, and this is the stronger of the two.

As you said, an encyclopedia's different, where a reader can happily go down a rabbit hole with a dozen tabs open, but I imagine that isn't a pleasant fiction reading experience for most (not all) readers.

I don't understand how giving the reader the option to do this (and doing so in a manner that does not require them to do so) is somehow undesirable. Nothing forces anyone to read crosslinks.

And, in any case, a wiki that deals with the problem of "Low readership on newer stuff" shouldn't be complaining about an effective and simple way to direct the attention of users to said content.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I understand the aversion to more than a couple crosslinks, as it can be seen as lazy - a reader wanting each piece to stand on its own, aside from a couple easter eggs. As you said, an encyclopedia's different, where a reader can happily go down a rabbit hole with a dozen tabs open, but I imagine that isn't a pleasant fiction reading experience for most (not all) readers. It doesn't bother me too much (no one's forcing me to click on them), but I get it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
First and foremost, it's dado. Not Dado. uppercase key broken.

the Foundation not analyzing all the products within the store because normally every spec of dust gets documented within an anomalous location like this.

I do explicitly state that this is a preliminary assessment. Tyrone's just there to case the joint, and the Foundation would use that information to decide how to proceed. Rest assured that if Eugene and Pius had not forced dado to close up shop, the Foundation would have conducted much more extensive tests, exploration and containment.

As for 3008, that was meant to be lampshade hanging. In-universe, Tyrone's been with the Foundation for years and has never been amnestisized because then his experience would be useless to them. He's seen a lot of crazy shit, and has heard about a lot more, including SCP-3008.

As for the crosslinks, what stormbreath said.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Then all the crosslinks. Normally I am a big fan of crosslinks but all of these feel very forced and almost worthless plus these are like, way too many. 11 crosslinks? That's a bit overkill.

I don't think you understand just how goddamn weird the SCP wiki's aversion to crosslinks is in comparison to any other wiki based website on the internet. If I were to go to any fandom wikia, I could expect to find many, many more crosslinks on the page. Go to any wiki and count the number of crosslinks. If they're anywhere near our size, you should expect upwards of 30 crosslinks - that's a low number.

I do concede that the SCP wiki is different from other wikis in that it is collaborative fiction rather an encyclopedia, but this is the closest example. The fact that eleven crosslinks is decried as overkill is patently absurd to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hot damn, every time.

I freely admit to giving a little cheer when Eugene and Pius arrived on scene, and a nice little grin when Ambrose turned up. Hell, I would read five hours worth of that log if it existed.

Can someone who was ambivalent about this skip give it a second +1 from me?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm old enough to remember the fad for 'generic' products (e.g., white cans of beer labeled BEER). This was like a dadofied version of that.

I was entertained, which is all I ask from dado SCPs. The idea that it had become a general destination for all manner of anomalous entities was well presented, and I was relieved that Tyrone got out all right.

Addendum: just listened to the audio. When it started it sounded like Popeye having a stroke and just went from there. dado's approach to singing is consistent with dado's approach to everything else.

I understand that dado is not everyone's cup of amino acid broth contaminated with rare earth minerals and hamster dander*, but then, not everyone enjoys anchovies, black licorice, headcheese and fruitcake either.

*u trust dado

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ever since the conception of Dado, I've figured out that it's nothing more than just fodder and set up for low tier humor. This article proves my point.

Aside from the fact that Dado literally rickrolls all those who enter his meme store, there are some things that feel very OOC for the Foundation such as a D-Class casually mentioning 3008 and the Foundation not analyzing all the products within the store because normally every spec of dust gets documented within an anomalous location like this. While I could let the 3008 thing slide with something about rumors spread umong the D-Class, the latter feels completely OOC.

Then all the crosslinks. Normally I am a big fan of crosslinks but all of these feel very forced and almost worthless plus these are like, way too many. 11 crosslinks? That's a bit overkill.

Overall, this is a subpar collage of memes and crosslinks that comes off as forced and falls flatter than a pancake.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm pretty sure insane pharmacist capable of teleporting his products everywhere with the IQ of a Markov Chain is already that.

item.title: u trust upvote
item.content:encoded: Dammit. I knew dadomart would happen some day. We need so much more dado on this site +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: who the fuck is "Dado?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I disliked and appreciated the same respective elements. The elements that aren't dado are good, but dado's pretty impossible to sell to me at this point.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
FWIW, I had much the same impression at first: dado is so fucking one-note, this should have been easy downvote fodder.

What absolutely sells this for me though is the depiction of a multiversal hub/bazaar/location teeming with all manner of eclectic characters from the most disparate of backgrounds.

The revolving door of authentic-feeling and zany character interactions was enough to offset my distaste for what it was wrapped in.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is all I've ever wanted from Dado.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What is a man to do when he stands before god

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I understand this was supposed to be funny, but I didn't find it remotely smile-worthy. Other than that, it was written well, but it got boring after I realized it was a dado SCP. I don't personally think dado is funny, but hey, others do. It is a -1 for me, sadly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: once long ago when dado keyboard was new, dado had very good caps lock key. whenever dado typed name or started sentence he turnd on the caps lock light, pressed the letbedbeter (sry hamster on keyboard) then turned off he little light. but one day light of capitalization stopped coming on after dado spild special cough syrup on keyboard. now dado uses caps keys friend shift key to make the parentheses

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Welcome to dado, the crossover episode. Complete with a cameo from Rick Astley himself apparently.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: grimdado

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: but do we really know what "sin" is

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Entertaining is the least of my compliments, but that audio killed anything I could've said.
Just take your damn +1 and run with it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Jump anomalous jump.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Jim Henson's The Labyrinth but set in a BJs and somehow on even more drugs.

ETA: which I guess means dado has the infamous Bowie Bulge.

item.title: What festivals of atonement, what sacred games shall we have to invent?
item.content:encoded:
If I could save time in a bottle
The first thing that I'd like to do
Is to save every day
Till Eternity passes away
Just to spend them with you

fuckles

+1 chandra you bastard genius you roped me into this horseshow.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I worked in a Toys 'R Us. I can confirm it was exactly like this, toilet paper and all.

+1, I'm a sucker for silly crossovers and general retail madness. I feel for the manager all too well, having worked with otherwise good dudes stuck working for chains.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Grimdark dado tale coming soon
